meta.name: AUDIT-C Alcohol Use Brief Screen
meta.fullName: Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption
meta.description: The AUDIT-C is a brief 3-item alcohol screening tool derived from the first 3 questions of the full AUDIT. It identifies hazardous drinking or active alcohol use disorders, and is widely used in primary care settings.
meta.author: Bush et al. (1998)
meta.reference: Bush K, Kivlahan DR, McDonell MB, Fihn SD, Bradley KA. The AUDIT alcohol consumption questions (AUDIT-C): an effective brief screening test for problem drinking. Arch Intern Med. 1998;158(16):1789-1795.
meta.instruction: Please answer the following questions about your alcohol use. Select the option that best describes your drinking habits.

questions.text: How often do you have a drink containing alcohol?
How many standard drinks containing alcohol do you have on a typical day when you are drinking?
How often do you have 6 or more drinks on one occasion?

ranges.label: Low Risk
ranges.description: Your alcohol consumption falls within the low-risk range. No significant concerns about problematic drinking habits were detected. Current patterns suggest minimal risk of alcohol-related health consequences.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your moderate or abstinent drinking habits
Stay mindful of situations or social settings where alcohol consumption may gradually increase
Keep informed about recommended daily and weekly drinking limits for your age and sex

ranges.label: Hazardous Drinking
ranges.description: Your drinking pattern suggests hazardous alcohol use that exceeds recommended limits and may progressively increase your risk for liver disease, injuries, mental health problems, and social difficulties.
suggestions: Consider reducing both the amount and frequency of your alcohol consumption starting this week
Track your drinks using a diary or app to develop greater awareness of your actual consumption
Set clear personal limits before social events and commit to non-alcoholic alternatives
Discuss your drinking patterns with a healthcare provider during your next routine visit

ranges.label: Harmful or Dependent Drinking
ranges.description: Your drinking pattern suggests harmful alcohol use or possible dependence. This level of consumption carries significant risk for liver damage, cardiovascular disease, and impaired daily functioning.
suggestions: Consulting a healthcare provider about your alcohol use is strongly recommended at this level
Consider seeking specialized addiction medicine or alcohol treatment services for assessment
Explore community resources such as support groups, counseling programs, or telephone helplines
Never drive or operate machinery after drinking — plan alternative transportation in advance
